methods.description: Preparation of the field site by digging step-like trenches and establishing spatial control for vertical and horizontal measurements of stratigraphic sections.

steps.description: We dug step-like trenches through the Chipalamawamba Beds to enable detailed stratigraphical and sedimentological descriptions and to measure and record the characteristics of individual beds.
steps.searchable_summary: Dig step-like trenches for stratigraphic access
outputs: Exposed stratigraphic section in a trench

steps.description: Vertical positions of trench steps and bedding planes were measured with an accuracy of ∼ 1 cm relative to the water level of the river (∼ 473-474 m a.s.l.). For this we used baseline nails, a theodolite (Sokkisha C3E, 22× optical magnification; accuracy ∼ 0.001 gon) and beacon.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure vertical elevation relative to a baseline using theodolite

steps.description: We kept a constant baseline by marking the water level with nails at each locality and comparing the positions of nails and the water level at previously established stations at the time of creating a baseline for a new locality.
steps.searchable_summary: Establish and maintain a consistent vertical baseline using water level and markers

steps.description: At each baseline nail GPS coordinates were taken with a Garmin GPS III Plus to allow calculation of distances between individual profiles.
steps.searchable_summary: Record GPS coordinates at survey points

steps.description: allow calculation of distances between individual profiles.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate distances between points using GPS coordinates

steps.description: Sometimes, distances between trenches and/or outcrops were directly measured using tape measure or with the theodolite and beacon.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure horizontal distances using tape or theodolite

steps.description: If required or desirable, additional semi-trenches were dug the next day to verify physical correlations or other aspects that proved ambiguous in the re-drawing phase.
steps.searchable_summary: Dig supplementary trenches for verification

methods.description: Detailed description and drawing of sedimentological and stratigraphical features observed in the trenches to construct lithostratigraphic profiles.

steps.description: For each section a lithostratigraphic profile was constructed.
steps.searchable_summary: Create a lithostratigraphic profile diagram

steps.description: Stratigraphical profiles were drawn on a 1/10 scale
steps.searchable_summary: Draw stratigraphic profiles to scale

steps.description: include information on grain size, sorting, sediment color, sedimentary structures, the fossil content of the beds and the nature of the stratigraphical contacts.
steps.searchable_summary: Annotate stratigraphic profiles with sedimentological and paleontological details

steps.description: Granulometry was determined by visual comparison with a grain chart (Krynine, 1948) at 10 × magnification
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate grain size using visual comparison with a chart

steps.description: colors were determined using the revised Standard Soil Color Charts (Oyoma and Takehara, 1967).
steps.searchable_summary: Determine sediment color using standard color charts
inputs: revised Standard Soil Color Charts (Oyoma and Takehara, 1967)

steps.description: profiles were re-drawn and correlated with earlier profiles.
steps.searchable_summary: Revise and correlate stratigraphic profiles based on new data

methods.description: Measurement of magnetic susceptibility and temperature directly on the exposed sediment profile in the trench at regular intervals.

steps.description: Every 5 cm, magnetic susceptibility (MS) was measured using a ZH instruments SM-30 meter at the shady side of the profile.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure magnetic susceptibility along a profile at fixed intervals

steps.description: measurements were performed with double calibration in the air (before and after).
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate magnetic susceptibility meter before and after measurements

steps.description: Each measurement was repeated three times
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat magnetic susceptibility measurements for replication
outputs: Multiple MS values for a single point

steps.description: if differences were larger than 0.40 × 10 −4 SI units, 2 additional measurements were taken.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform additional magnetic susceptibility measurements if initial readings vary significantly
outputs: Additional MS values for a single point

steps.description: As MS is temperature-dependent, temperatures were measured for every step in the trench.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure temperature along the profile during MS measurement

methods.description: Collection of bulk sediment samples from the stratigraphic profile at regular intervals for subsequent laboratory analysis.

steps.description: Sediment samples were taken every 5 or 10 cm with tubes of ∼ 3 cm 3 for later sedimentological analyses in the lab.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect bulk sediment samples at fixed intervals using tubes

steps.description: At locality L1 a continuous sediment profile was taken as well, but as this method proved suboptimal (and highly time-consuming) in the loosely aggregated sandy sediments at Chipalamawamba, it was not performed for other trenches.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect a continuous sediment core/profile sample

methods.description: Collection and initial processing of fossil material and shell beds found in the trenches and outcrops.

steps.description: During fieldwork in 2008 we mainly aimed to collect fossils from outcrops
steps.searchable_summary: Collect fossils from exposed outcrops

steps.description: Shell beds, when present, were sampled individually in a qualitative and/or quantitative way
steps.searchable_summary: Sample shell beds qualitatively or quantitatively

steps.description: (by weighing the sample
steps.searchable_summary: Weigh quantitative shell bed samples

steps.description: and subsequently sieving it using mesh sizes of 2 mm and 710 µm).
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve shell bed samples using specified mesh sizes

steps.description: Taphonomic features were recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: Record taphonomic characteristics of shell beds

steps.description: In situ samples of fossils were collected during the digging of the trenches only if the exact stratigraphical position could be established.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect in-situ fossils with recorded stratigraphic position
outputs: In-situ fossil specimens with position data

steps.description: However, fossils of uncertain positions were screened
steps.searchable_summary: Screen fossils with uncertain stratigraphic positions

steps.description: and when they displayed interesting or unusual features, they were bagged as ex situ.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect ex-situ fossils based on specific features

steps.description: Otherwise, material was only collected during the cleaning and profile-drawing phases.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect fossils found during profile cleaning and drawing

steps.description: Quantitative samples were sieved after dark
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve quantitative samples (general, likely shell beds)
